briefs.regime_note: FOMO-extended; tech and logistics rolling, energy/healthcare/staples bid

headline.plain: Stocks open soft. Shipping stocks like UPS and FedEx down sharply on demand fears. Energy and healthcare still leading.
headline.pro: SPY/QQQ flat open, RSI cooling. UPS -6.4, FDX -5.6 lead logistics breakdown. XLE +13.6 leads, XLK -9.0 rolls. AMD/ARM Wed AMC.

market_status.text: Regular session. Closing bell in 6h 21m at 16:00 ET.

top_actions.text: Shipping breakdown live: UPS -6.4, FDX -5.6, CHRW -4.5, ODFL -4.3 all rolling on consumer demand fears.

detail.plain: Major shipping companies dropped 4 to 6 percent at the open. UPS and FedEx led the breakdown. This is often an early warning sign about consumer spending, since shipping volume tracks demand directly. Wait for a bounce and a rejection candle before fading these names; do not catch the falling knife.
detail.pro: Logistics universe broken at the open: UPS -6.37 (101), FDX -5.56 (372), CHRW -4.45 (169), EXPD -3.91 (141), ODFL -4.28 (197), NCLH -4.92 (18). Sector implication: consumer/freight demand softening; XLI dropped from leadership group. Failed-bounce short setups will likely emerge in 1-3 sessions on first relief candle. Vehicle: debit put spreads 30-45 DTE on rejection.

top_actions.text: Sell-put-spread regime is on; structural contango plus calm spot favors income trades.

detail.plain: Volatility is sleepy and the futures market is paying you to sit in a defined-risk short volatility position. The trade is to sell a put spread on VIX, where the most you can lose is capped. The expiry suggested is mid-June and the strikes are 17 short, 15 long. This is an income trade, not a directional bet.
detail.pro: VIX 17.70 spot, contango steep, 6/17 17P/15P credit estimate 0.38. Trade: STO VIX 2026-06-17 17P, BTO VIX 2026-06-17 15P. Max risk 200 per spread, size max 1 spread per 5k equity. Exit 50 percent of credit or within 7 DTE. Roll-down tailwind compounds with theta.

top_actions.text: Mechanical short trigger fired but Friday closed green; wait for a rejection candle Monday before acting.

detail.plain: PODD broke down on earnings and bounced back into the zone where a short trade is set up. The system flagged it as triggered, but Friday was a green day so the bounce is still going. Real trade requires a red rejection candle in the 170 to 175 zone before entering. Check Monday close before doing anything.
detail.pro: PODD last 175.04, RSI 33.6, 20MA 192.99, ATR 7.62, FOMO ceiling 208.16. Mechanical TRIGGERED status fired (last greater than 170) but thesis-level requires lower-high plus red close inside the zone. If Mon prints a rejection candle, vehicle is debit put spread 30-45 DTE around 170/160. Stop on close above 183. First target 145, second 135. Spread mid bid-ask must be under 10 percent before fill.

top_actions.text: Index rule blocks new long entries today: SPY and QQQ are above 20-day average plus 2 ATR.

detail.plain: Both major indexes have run too far too fast. The buying rule says wait for a pullback before adding new long positions. This is not a sell signal; existing holdings stay put. It just means do not chase breakouts at these levels.
detail.pro: SPY 720.65 RSI 79.1, QQQ 674.15 RSI 82.8, both above 20MA plus 2 ATR. FOMO rule active: deterministic block on long entries until pullback to 20MA or below. LEAP signal WAIT for both, RSI extended. VIX put spread is the only regime-permitted directional today.

indices.note: ATH, above all MAs, RSI extreme

detail.plain: S and P 500 ETF closed Friday at a record high. The relative strength index is 79, which is well above the 70 line that traders watch as overheated. Pullbacks of 3 to 5 percent are the typical reset from this level.
detail.pro: SPY 720.65, +0.94 pct 5d, RSI14 79.1, ATR14 6.73. All MAs aligned bullish but stretched. 20MA at 698.5, 1 ATR pullback target 713-714.

indices.note: ATH, RSI 82.8 most extreme of the three

detail.plain: Nasdaq 100 ETF is the most stretched of the major indexes. RSI at 82.8 is rare and historically precedes a cooling-off period. Tech stocks specifically have been rotating out at the sector level.
detail.pro: QQQ 674.15, +1.55 pct 5d, RSI14 82.8, ATR14 9.0. 20MA 638.20. Sector rotation: XLK -9.06 5d, the worst leg in the wheel. Mean reversion target 645-655.

indices.note: Small caps catching up but less stretched than QQQ

detail.plain: Russell 2000 ETF is also overbought but less extreme than tech. Small caps usually move together with the broader market and respond strongly to interest rate moves.
detail.pro: IWM 279.28, +0.95 pct 5d, RSI14 72.3, ATR14 4.17. Less FOMO-extended than QQQ. Yield-sensitive: 10Y 4.38, watch ISM Services Tue.

detail.plain: Energy sector ETF leads the market over the last week. Oil up 7 percent in 5 days is the underlying driver. This kind of rotation often persists for 2 to 4 weeks once it starts.
detail.pro: XLE rotation score plus 13.1, oil +7.19 pct 5d. Watchlist long entry below 56.50 still needs a flush; price 58.85 sits 4.2 pct above trigger. Confirms commodity bid against tech rollover.

detail.plain: Healthcare sector ETF is second strongest. Defensive bid suggests money rotating out of risk and into stable cash-flow names.
detail.pro: XLV rotation +10.75. Confirms defensive bid (XLP and XLU nearby). HUM watchlist setup is the cleanest defensive long expression: long-on-pullback below 228 with reversal candle, target 267, R:R 3.4 to 1.

detail.plain: Utilities sector is bid alongside healthcare. This is a classic late-cycle defensive rotation pattern.
detail.pro: XLU rotation +9.29. Yield-driven plus AI power-demand secular bid. No specific entry without pullback below 20MA.

detail.plain: Tech sector ETF lags badly in the last 5 days. This is the loudest signal in the market right now: money is leaving tech and going to energy and defensives. Watch for whether it continues into next week or reverses.
detail.pro: XLK rotation -9.06, the largest single-sector laggard. Confirms 5d rotation regime. Tech-heavy holders: this is the early read on whether to trim or hedge. NDX-heavy equity portfolios face headwind unless XLK reclaims its 20MA.

detail.plain: Consumer discretionary ETF is barely positive over 5 days. Slight rollover signals consumer caution.
detail.pro: XLY rotation -0.80. Modest weakness; not a primary signal. Watch RIVN -8.41 pct Friday and CLX -9.67 as discretionary distress markers.

sector_rotation.read: Defensive plus energy bid against tech rollover is the cleanest 5-day signal. If it persists Mon-Wed, mean-revert trades into XLE and XLV outperform breakout chases.

events_14d.event: Fed Williams Speech, Factory Orders MoM (Mar)

detail.plain: Fed President John Williams speaks Monday. Markets will scan for any change in tone about cuts. Factory orders is a mild data point.
detail.pro: Williams typically dovish-leaning; if he flags labor softness it is a relief tape. Factory Orders consensus low single digit; Mar print marginal market mover.

events_14d.event: ISM Services PMI (Apr), Fed Barr Speech, EIA Crude Inventories

detail.plain: Tuesday is the bigger calendar day. Services PMI is the largest piece of US data this week. A reading above 52 means services growing strong; below 50 means contracting. Fed Barr also speaks.
detail.pro: ISM Services consensus 51.5 (last 51.7). Above 53 means yields up means tech selloff deepens. Below 50 means bond rally plus tech bid back. Watch 10Y reaction first. Crude inv: oil-sensitive; XLE setup hinges on it.

events_14d.event: ARM and AMD earnings AMC, ALB/AMCR/APA earnings

detail.plain: ARM Holdings and AMD report earnings after the close. Expect implied volatility to rise into the print and crash after. AI semiconductor names move on these reports.
detail.pro: ARM and AMD ER 5/06 AMC, IV crush probable post-print. Pre-earnings IV expansion in AAPL/AVGO/SNDK chains likely from sympathy.

detail.plain: Weekly jobs data and a couple of large-cap names report Thursday.
detail.pro: Claims consensus high 200s; persistent uptick in claims has been the dovish-bias swing factor for weeks.

earnings_7d.note: AI semi tone-setter; auto-blackout zone active for holders

detail.plain: ARM Holdings is one of the biggest AI chip-design names. The reaction sets the tone for the whole AI semiconductor group. Holders should plan whether to hold through the print or trim into the spike in implied volatility.
detail.pro: ARM ER 5/06 AMC. IV crush risk sized at 25-40 pct on options legs. Long-call holders: scale out 50 pct into IV expansion 24h pre-print or buy short-dated put hedge for tail. Alternative: sell a covered call on the spike for income.

earnings_7d.note: Southeast Asia ride-hailing/payments; LEAP holders typically hold through

detail.plain: Grab Holdings is a Southeast Asian ride-hailing and payments company. The stock has been a chronic underperformer with high volatility around earnings. LEAP holders typically hold through given the long-dated nature.
detail.pro: GRAB last 3.67, RSI 47.1, 20MA 3.85, ATR 0.16. Short-dated 3.50P is the cheap tail hedge if expected move blows. LEAP delta exposure is low; downside is bounded by position size.

detail.plain: Albemarle is the largest lithium producer in the US. Lithium prices have been compressed; any guidance on demand recovery moves the stock.
detail.pro: ALB cyclical lithium proxy. Watch RIVN/TSLA aftermarket reaction; sympathy through XLB and EV-supplier basket.

detail.plain: McDonalds gives a read on the global consumer. Same-store sales and US traffic are the main numbers to watch.
detail.pro: MCD consumer health read; defensive in name but cyclical in traffic. Same-store sales surprise drives XLY through-read.

action.text: Macro day Mon-Tue. Williams plus Barr plus ISM Services define whether the rotation persists or reverses.

buy.confidence_reason: Energy is the cleanest 5-day rotation winner with oil up 7 pct, but entry is 4 pct above the trigger and a flush below 56.50 is still required.
buy.pros: Strongest sector rotation in the universe at +13.1, sustained two days. Oil leadership at +7.19 pct 5d confirms underlying commodity bid. XLE base built 54-55 holds and is bouncing toward 20MA reclaim, implying a clean 56.50 to 59.50 trade with R:R 2.14.
buy.cons: XLE last 58.85 is well above the 56.50 entry trigger; chasing here gives away most of the edge. Oil could reverse on unexpected inventory build Tuesday. A close below 54.50 invalidates the setup entirely.

action.plain: Set a price alert at 56.50. If energy ETF pulls back into that zone, buy 100 to 200 shares with a stop at 55. Target around 59.50.
action.pro: Search XLE. Set price alert at 56.50. On hit: Buy Limit at mid, GFD. Stop-Limit GTC at 55.10 limit 54.85. Target Limit GTC at 59.50.

buy.confidence_reason: Spot VIX 17.5 with steep contango plus 3M 20.37 gives structural roll-down tailwind, and the trade is the only deterministic-rule-permitted directional today.
buy.pros: Structural VIX contango at +2.87 pp creates a roll-down tailwind that compounds with theta. Vol regime is calm with steep contango; spot 17.5 sits in the 14-22 normal-calm corridor where short-vol historically pays. Defined-risk credit spread caps loss if vol spikes.
buy.cons: Single fat-tail vol spike erases multiple months of credit; max risk is 5x the credit. VIX options are settled to a unique cash-settled calculation; assignment math differs from equity options. If spot VIX collapses below 15, the trade can pin or reverse on long-strike pressure.

action.plain: Sell a put spread on VIX expiring June 17. The max you can lose is fixed and small. The goal is for VIX to stay above 17, where you keep most of the credit.
action.pro: Search VIX. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-17. Sell to Open 1 VIX 17P, Buy to Open 1 VIX 15P. Limit at net credit 0.40 mid, GFD. Max risk 200 per spread, size max 1 spread per 5k equity. Exit at 50 pct of max credit OR within 7 DTE.

sell.confidence_reason: Tech is the worst 5-day sector laggard at -9.06 with QQQ RSI 82.8, but mega-cap earnings are still pending so the rotation could reverse on a single AAPL or NVDA print.
sell.pros: XLK is the largest sector laggard 5d at -9.06. QQQ RSI 82.8 is among the most extended readings of the year, and rotation regimes typically last 2-4 weeks. Defensive plus energy bid suggests money is actively leaving tech, not just rotating within.
sell.cons: Mega-cap earnings season is mid-cycle and a single AAPL or NVDA print can reverse the rotation overnight. Trimming a winning sector at a top is the highest-regret pattern in active management. XLK could reclaim 20MA quickly if ISM Services prints soft and yields fall.

action.plain: If you have a heavy tech position and have not trimmed in months, peel back 10 to 15 percent into strength. This is risk control, not a directional sell call.
action.pro: If overweight XLK or NDX-style names: scale out 10-15 pct into Mon strength. Use Limit at last + 0.5 pct, GFD. Reassess on a close below 20MA. Hedge alternative: SQQQ debit call spread 30 DTE 35/40.

detail.plain: AMD reports the same day as ARM. Both reports will dominate AI semi sentiment for the week. Spreads and short-dated trades crystallize the IV cycle.
detail.pro: AMD 5/06 AMC. Likely +/- 7 pct expected move. Defined-risk: debit call spread or put spread sized to the implied move. Avoid naked premium; theta and IV crush both work against long options.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: Best technical health (RSI 60.9, no earnings, above 200MA) with a 60.9 pct annualized yield, capped because it tracks oil futures and contango/backwardation can erode NAV mid-cycle.
wheel_candidates.pros: United States Oil Fund tracks WTI futures and is in a clean uptrend with energy sector leadership at +13.1 5d. RSI 60.9 is healthy without being extreme; no earnings catalyst. Annualized yield 60.9 pct is the highest of the score-1.00 cohort. Cash-secured put at 134 sits 6.16 pct below spot, with ATR at 4.89 pct giving plenty of cushion.
wheel_candidates.cons: USO is a futures-tracking ETF; persistent contango erodes NAV over time even when oil prices hold. Geopolitical reversal in Mid-East tension can cause oil to gap 5 pct in either direction overnight. If assigned, the underlying carries roll-yield decay rather than a real corporate cash flow.

action.plain: Sell one cash-secured put on the United States Oil Fund. Strike 134, expiring early June. You collect about 715 dollars upfront. If the fund falls below 134 by expiry, you buy 100 shares at 134. Plan to sell covered calls if assigned.
action.pro: Search USO. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-05. Sell to Open 1 USO 134P. Limit at net credit 7.15 mid, GFD. Collateral 13,400. Stop alert at 130 underlying. If assigned, write a 142C 30-45 DTE for income.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: Strong technical health and no current ER blackout (30 days out), but starting position concentration in a name where direct equity exposure may already exist caps confidence.
wheel_candidates.pros: Broadcom is a high-quality AI semi name with a strong cash flow profile and steady dividend. RSI 69.5 is firm without being extreme; ATR 2.9 pct is among the lower in the cohort. CSP at 395 is 6.24 pct OTM with a 13.44 premium, annualized 38.8 pct.
wheel_candidates.cons: Earnings in 30 days means this CSP will straddle the pre-earnings IV expansion window. Cash collateral of 39,500 ties up significant capital. AI-semi rotation OUT (XLK -9.06 5d) is the largest sector laggard and a continuation lasts weeks.

action.plain: Sell a cash-secured put on Broadcom at strike 395, expiring early June. Collateral required is 39,500. You collect about 1,344 in premium. Close at 50 percent profit or roll to next month if needed.
action.pro: Search AVGO. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-05. Sell to Open 1 AVGO 395P. Limit at net credit 13.44 mid, GFD. Collateral 39,500. Stop alert at 410. Close at 50 pct of max credit OR roll one expiry forward if assignment risk rises.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: 76.5 pct annualized yield is exceptional and earnings are 51 days out, but RSI 78 plus parabolic structure and ATR 5.34 pct flag elevated tail risk if the storage rotation snaps back.
wheel_candidates.pros: Micron is the secular AI memory winner; storage cycle is the loudest sector theme in the universe. Earnings 51 days out is well clear of the wheel scanner penalty zone. 76.5 pct annualized yield is among the best in the cohort.
wheel_candidates.cons: RSI 78 with parabolic structure means a single -10 pct day is realistic. ATR 5.34 pct is the second-widest after USO; collateral 51,000 ties up serious capital. If sector rotation OUT of tech persists, MU is the highest-beta name in the wheel.

action.plain: Sell a cash-secured put on Micron at strike 510, expiring early June. Premium is rich at about 3,421 dollars but the collateral is 51,000 and the position is volatile. Only do this if you have the cash and are OK owning more Micron.
action.pro: Search MU. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-05. Sell to Open 1 MU 510P. Limit at net credit 34.21 mid, GFD. Collateral 51,000. Stop alert at 525. Close at 50 pct of max credit. If assigned, immediately write 540C or 550C 30 DTE for income.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: Solid 41 pct yield with a defensive setup, but earnings are 16 days out which sits just outside the 14-day penalty band and can still produce a binary outcome.
wheel_candidates.pros: Target is a defensive consumer name with steady cash flow. ATR 2.36 pct is the lowest in the cohort, meaning collateral risk is contained. CSP at 122 with 5.35 pct cushion is reasonable; defensive XLV plus XLP bid lifts consumer staples adjacents.
wheel_candidates.cons: Earnings in 16 days is just outside the 14-day penalty band and the print is binary. CLX -9.67 pct Friday is the leading-edge consumer-staples breakdown that could spread; same-store sales surprise risk is real. RSI 73.4 limits upside cushion.

action.plain: Sell a cash-secured put on Target at strike 122 expiring early June. Premium is small at about 440 dollars. Collateral 12,200. Earnings are 16 days out, so close before the print to avoid binary surprise.
action.pro: Search TGT. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-05. Sell to Open 1 TGT 122P. Limit at net credit 4.40 mid, GFD. Collateral 12,200. Stop alert at 124. CLOSE before ER 5/20 to avoid binary print. Target 50 pct of max credit.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: 82 pct yield is the cohort high, but RSI 87.2 (extreme) reflects a vertical post-earnings rip that has high mean-revert probability.
wheel_candidates.pros: Intel ripped 23 pct on earnings 4/30 and held the gap on Friday. 82 pct annualized yield is the cohort high. Earnings are 80 days away, well clear. The post-earnings continuation pattern (PEAD-long) historically holds for 30-60 days when the initial gap is this large.
wheel_candidates.cons: RSI 87.2 is an extreme reading and vertical post-earnings rips like this have a high probability of a -10 pct mean-revert session within 5-10 sessions. The 23 pct gap can fill quickly if the AI-foundry guidance disappoints in detail. ATR 5.46 pct means the 94 strike can be tagged on a single -5 pct day.

action.plain: Sell a cash-secured put on Intel at strike 94 expiring early June. Premium is 676. Collateral 9,400. The stock is very stretched after the earnings rip; only do this if you would be happy owning Intel at 94.
action.pro: Search INTC. Trade Options. Expiration 2026-06-05. Sell to Open 1 INTC 94P. Limit at net credit 6.76 mid, GFD. Collateral 9,400. Stop alert at 92. Close at 50 pct of max credit OR if Intel closes below 95.

action.text: Income trades only today: VIX put spread plus cash-secured put on USO is the cleanest paired setup. AAPL spread waits for pullback.

buy.confidence_reason: Three-source cluster supports buying the spread on a pullback to 275-278, but FOMO-extended index keeps the score below decision-grade.
buy.pros: AAPL three-source confirmation (mover_active plus unusual_flow plus breakout). RSI 69.3 is firm; MAs stack bullishly under spot. WWDC catalyst window opens early June and AI-hardware narrative is in bid.
buy.cons: Tech rotation OUT at -9.06 5d means even good chart setups face a sector tide. Spread is the right vehicle (defined risk plus leverage) but entering at 280 chasing leaves no margin if the index pulls back.

action.plain: Set an alert at 275 on Apple. If it pulls back to that area, buy a 285 call and sell a 300 call expiring late May. The most you can lose is the cost of the spread.
action.pro: Search AAPL. Set alert at 275. Trade Options 2026-05-30. Buy to Open AAPL 285C, Sell to Open AAPL 300C. Limit at net debit mid, GFD. Stop on close below 270. Target 50-75 pct of max profit.

sell.confidence_reason: Setup zone is hit and structure favors shorting, but Friday's green close means rejection candle is missing.
sell.pros: PODD broke down on volume 4.9x average on 4/29; pre-breakdown support at 183 has flipped to resistance; medical device peer weakness (SYK -6.47) confirms sector breakdown. R:R approx 4:1.
sell.cons: Friday closed +1.68 pct green which means the bounce is still extending. Defensive sector tailwind XLV +10.75 5d could lift broken names. M and A speculation in defensive medtech is endemic.

action.plain: Wait for PODD to print a red rejection day in the 170 to 175 zone. Then buy a 170/160 put spread expiring late June. Stop on close above 183.
action.pro: Search PODD. Wait for rejection candle (lower high plus red close) in 170-175. Trade Options 2026-06-20. Buy to Open PODD 170P, Sell to Open PODD 160P. Limit at net debit mid, GFD. Stop on close above 183. Target 50-75 pct of max profit.

headline.plain: Stocks at record highs but moving too fast. AMD and Arm earnings Wednesday.
headline.pro: Indices at ATHs but RSI extended; AMD/ARM print Wed sets AI-semi tone.

market_status.text: Pre-market trading. Opening bell in 5h 00m at 09:30 ET.

top_actions.text: Short setup is live in the $170 to $175 zone. Need a Monday rejection candle before sizing.

detail.plain: Insulet (PODD) makes insulin pumps for diabetics. The chart suggests it's heading lower over the next few weeks. We're watching for it to bounce up toward $170 to $175 on Monday and then start falling. That's the cue. If you're new to this, the simpler move is to watch this week and see how it plays out. The company reports earnings Wednesday morning, so anything you do has to happen Monday or Tuesday before that report.
detail.pro: PODD reports earnings Wednesday before open. Crossed into the short trigger zone Friday but closed green inside the zone, so the setup is unconfirmed. Need a Monday upper-wick candle closing in the lower third. Robinhood: search PODD, Trade Options, May 8 weekly $170 put, Buy to Open, Limit at mid, Good for day. Stop is a price alert at $176.50, close the put if it triggers. Skip the trade if Monday closes green again.

top_actions.text: Wednesday earnings after the close. AMD prints same evening and together they set the AI semi tone for the week.

detail.plain: Arm Holdings (ARM) designs the chip technology used in nearly every smartphone, including iPhones. They report quarterly results on Wednesday after the market closes. The stock is expected to swing about 11% up or down based on the report. If you're new to this, the safest move is to wait until Thursday and see how the market reacts before doing anything. Trying to guess the report is roughly a coin flip, and even if you're right on direction, options can lose value just from the report being out.
detail.pro: First print since the licensing-model concerns. Stock is up 8.7% on the week into the print. Implied move is 11%, so options premium is rich. Best vehicles: defined-risk debit call spread, or sit out and trade the post-print reaction. Robinhood bull spread: search ARM, Trade Options, May 16, Buy 215C and Sell 230C, Limit a small net debit, Good for day. Max loss is the debit, max profit is $15 per spread minus debit. Don't size larger than 1% of account.

top_actions.text: SPY and QQQ both more than 2 ATR above their 20-day MA. New long entries are statistically late.

detail.plain: The overall market (S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100) is at record highs and has gone up sharply for several weeks without a pause. When stocks go up this fast in a row, they usually take a breather, a 3% to 5% drop, before going higher again. If you already own broad index funds, just hold them. If you were thinking about adding more, wait for that breather. There's nothing to do here today. This is a sit-on-hands moment.
detail.pro: SPY 720, QQQ 674. Both at 52w highs with RSI above 79. Entries from this distance historically underperform a pullback entry by 60 to 80 percent over the next 10 sessions. Action is to wait. If you already hold index ETFs, the FOMO gate doesn't say sell, it says don't add. Reset levels: SPY 698, QQQ 638 (rising 20MA touch). That's where new longs get back on.

indices.note: Above all moving averages, at 52-week high.

detail.plain: SPY is the most popular fund that tracks the 500 biggest US companies. It just hit an all-time high. Recent gains have been very fast. Historically when this happens, the market tends to take a small breather of 3% to 5% within the next two weeks. That doesn't mean sell. It means don't rush to add more right now.
detail.pro: RSI 79.1 is the highest reading since November 2024. The last three crosses above RSI 78 saw 3 to 5 percent pullbacks within 10 sessions. Position-sizing signal, not a sell signal. Watch the 20MA at 698, the first logical support and reset level for new entries.

detail.plain: QQQ tracks the 100 biggest tech-focused companies on Nasdaq, including Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, and Meta. It's at an all-time high and even more stretched than SPY. Tech has been the leader, but it's getting tired. If you own it, fine. If you were planning to buy more, wait for a pullback toward $668 first.
detail.pro: QQQ RSI 82.8, more extreme than SPY. Tech leadership intact but stretched. Watch 668 Monday, the rising 5-day MA and the first logical support if profit-taking begins. Strong holds are fine, new entries are not.

detail.plain: IWM tracks 2,000 small-cap US companies. Small caps had been lagging the big names, so it's a positive sign that they're finally joining the rally. But the move is only a few days old, so it's still fragile. We need to see them keep leading for another two weeks before this becomes a sustainable trend.
detail.pro: Small caps finally joining is breadth-positive. RSI 72 with only 4 sessions of leadership is fragile. Need 2 weeks of relative strength versus SPY to confirm rotation. No new IWM entries above $280 until then.

detail.plain: XLE is the energy sector fund. It holds Exxon, Chevron, and other oil and gas companies. Energy stocks have been the best performers this past week. Often when energy leads while everything else is at highs, it's a sign the rally is getting late. Worth watching, not chasing.
detail.pro: Energy outperformance at indices ATHs is unusual, typical of late-cycle rotation. Watch crude above $103 to confirm the bid is real. If you want exposure: XLE pullback to $56.50 (rising 20MA) is the cleaner entry, not chasing $58.85.

detail.plain: XLP is the consumer staples fund. It holds Procter & Gamble, Coca-Cola, Walmart, things people buy regardless of the economy. When safe stocks like these lead a hot market, it's a quiet warning sign that big investors are getting cautious.
detail.pro: Defensive group leading at indices ATHs is a textbook risk-off tell. Treat XLP leadership as a yellow flag for the broader risk trade, not a reason to chase staples here.

detail.plain: XLRE tracks real estate companies (REITs). They tend to do well when interest rates fall. This one is leading because the market expects the Fed to cut rates later this year, not because real estate fundamentals are great.
detail.pro: Rate-sensitive group bidding up means rates are expected to fall. Real estate up but lagging fundamentals, caught between the rate tailwind and weak commercial demand. More signal about bond market expectations than about real estate.

detail.plain: XLK is the broad tech sector. It's lagging this week even though Nasdaq is at highs. That means a few giant names like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia are doing all the work while smaller tech is selling off. Narrow rallies like this often don't last.
detail.pro: XLK weakness while QQQ prints highs equals mega-caps carrying. Equal-weight tech is selling off underneath. The dispersion is the story. Watch breadth Monday: if equal-weight sells more, expect mega-caps to follow within 5 sessions.

detail.plain: XLY is consumer discretionary. It holds Amazon, Tesla, Home Depot, things people buy when they feel financially confident. It's flat to down this week while safety stocks are leading. That's a sign consumers may be tightening up. Watch this Friday's consumer-sentiment report.
detail.pro: Discretionary flat-to-down at ATHs while staples lead is a consumer-spending warning. Watch UMich Sentiment Friday for confirmation. If sentiment slips and discretionary keeps lagging, that's a recession-tilt signal.

detail.plain: XLU is utility companies. They've lagged this week. Nothing actionable here on its own.
detail.pro: Utilities lagging while real estate leads is unusual since both are rate-sensitive. Likely a function of the AI-power-demand narrative cooling. Not actionable on its own.

sector_rotation.read: Late-cycle rotation. Energy and staples leading, tech and discretionary lagging despite ATHs is a defensive tilt under the surface.

detail.plain: John Williams runs the New York branch of the Federal Reserve and helps decide US interest rate policy. He's giving a speech Monday. What he says will move the market a bit. If he hints at lower rates, stocks tend to go up; if he sounds tougher on inflation, stocks dip. Nothing for you to do, just be aware the market may bounce around.
detail.pro: Williams is NY Fed President, a core-Fed centrist. Watch for any pushback on the market's three-cuts-by-year-end pricing. Hawkish, brief equity dip and bond yield pop. Dovish, the bid continues. No trade, just calibrate Tuesday's data expectations.

detail.plain: A monthly report on how many new orders US factories are getting. It's a measure of how strong the economy is. If the number is weaker than expected, the market may rally on hopes of rate cuts. Probably a small move, not big enough to act on alone.
detail.pro: Consensus +0.3%. A negative print would be the third in five months and would steepen the yield curve (bond-bullish, growth-bearish). A beat reinforces the soft-landing narrative.

detail.plain: Big investors bought a huge amount of put options on the S&P 500 fund Friday. Puts pay off if the market goes down. This is mostly insurance. They're protecting their large stock holdings, not betting on a crash. But it shows nerves at the top.
detail.pro: Vol/OI 5618× on 5/4 $732P, spot 720.65. OTM put hedging into Monday, protection not directional. Standard month-end hedge with indices at ATHs. Confirms the market is nervous up here, not a tradeable signal alone.

detail.plain: Heavy options buying on Nvidia call options expiring this Friday at the $202.50 strike. Calls pay off if the stock goes up. This is bullish positioning ahead of AMD's report Wednesday. Nvidia tends to move with AMD on AI news. Don't copy this trade if you're new, too short-dated and risky.
detail.pro: Bullish positioning into AMD's Wed print (NVDA moves in sympathy on AI semi tone). Play: small May 16 debit call spread (Buy 200C / Sell 210C). Don't naked-long NVDA calls into AMD's print, IV crush will eat the premium even if NVDA goes up.

detail.plain: Concentrated put activity on Tesla for Monday expiry. Probably a single large hedge against unexpected delivery data, not a directional bet.
detail.pro: Single-strike Monday put concentration. Likely hedge against an unexpected delivery print or a general profit-protection trade. Not actionable alone.

detail.plain: After Apple ran 3.3% Friday, big holders bought protective options to lock in the gain without selling shares. Sign that even insiders think AAPL is short-term overbought.
detail.pro: Hedging the gain, not betting on a crash. Probably fund managers locking in profits without selling shares. Confirms AAPL is overbought near-term.

detail.plain: Same story as the SPY put activity above. Large players buying near-the-money insurance on the Nasdaq 100. Defensive, not predictive.
detail.pro: Tech-specific hedge buying ahead of Monday open. Spot $674, hedge at $676P is near-the-money, institutional protection on the long-only book. Not directional.

earnings_iv.note: AI semi sentiment driver. IV elevated. Implied move 9.5%.

detail.plain: Options on AMD are pricing a roughly 9.5% move in the stock by Friday. That's expensive but not unreasonable for an AI chip company at earnings. If you're new, just observe and trade the post-print direction Thursday morning.
detail.pro: IV-implied move 9.5%, rich vs AMD's avg earnings move (6%). Best vehicles: 5/16 debit call spread (Buy 365C / Sell 380C) for upside, or short iron condor if you think the move stays inside. Avoid naked long calls, IV crush eats 30 to 50% of premium even on a beat.

earnings_iv.note: Wednesday print. IV elevated. Implied move 11%.

detail.plain: Options on ARM are pricing about an 11% move in the stock, even more expensive than AMD. If you don't already trade options regularly, this is a watch-only situation.
detail.pro: Implied move 11%. Tighter spreads in May 8 weeklies than May 16 monthlies, calendar play (sell weekly, buy monthly) makes sense if you have a directional view and want IV-crush exposure.

action.text: AMD and ARM IV is rich Wednesday. Defined-risk debit spreads only, naked options bleed to IV crush even on a beat.

buy.pros: Wednesday earnings, whisper number above consensus on data-center. Defined risk caps your loss to the debit.
buy.cons: Implied move is 9.5%. Even on a beat, IV crush hits the long leg. Bar is already priced high.

action.plain: Buy the spread for a small debit ($2 to $4 typical for these widths). Max loss is what you pay. Max profit is $15 minus the debit per spread.
action.pro: Robinhood: search AMD, Trade Options, May 16 expiry, tap 365 (Buy to Open Call), tap 380 (Sell to Open Call), Limit your net debit, GFD. Size under 1% of account.

buy.confidence_reason: Defined-risk structure caps loss to debit and the data-center whisper is above consensus, capped because IV crush hits the long leg even on a beat and the bar is already priced high.

buy.pros: Pre-earnings strength suggests positioning. Defined risk via the spread caps your loss.
buy.cons: Implied move 11%, premium expensive. First print since licensing concerns, guidance is wildcard.

action.plain: Same setup as AMD. Buy the spread for a small debit, size small.
action.pro: Robinhood: search ARM, Trade Options, May 16, Buy 215C and Sell 230C, Limit small net debit, GFD.

buy.confidence_reason: Same defined-risk logic as AMD with pre-earnings strength supporting the bull thesis, capped because 11% implied move is rich and royalty-rate guidance is unresolved.

buy.pros: Sympathy play on AMD's Wednesday print. NVDA moves with AMD on AI semi tone.
buy.cons: Two layers of risk: AMD has to beat AND NVDA has to follow. Stop is tight.

action.plain: Smaller-size spread. If AMD beats Wednesday after-hours, NVDA gaps up Thursday. The spread captures part of that move with capped loss.
action.pro: Robinhood: search NVDA, Trade Options, May 16, Buy 200C and Sell 210C, Limit small net debit, GFD. Size under 0.5% of account given the indirect setup.

buy.confidence_reason: Sympathy play on AMD's print with two layers of risk (AMD has to beat AND NVDA has to follow), so the score stays low.

sell.pros: Watchlist short trigger live. Earnings Wednesday before open is the catalyst window.
sell.cons: Friday closed green, setup unconfirmed. Premium decays fast on a 5-day option.

action.plain: Wait for Monday rejection candle. If it prints, buy the put. If Monday closes green again, skip the trade entirely.
action.pro: Robinhood: search PODD, Trade Options, May 8 weekly, Buy 170P, Limit at mid, GFD. Stop alert $176.50.

sell.confidence_reason: Watchlist short trigger live with earnings catalyst Wed before open, capped because Monday closed green inside the zone and the 5-day option decays fast.

sell.ticker: Avoid naked long calls into earnings (any ticker)
sell.pros: If you're new to options, the most common loss pattern is buying a call before earnings, the company beats, and the option still loses value because IV collapses.
sell.cons: There's a real chance you're right on direction and still lose money. Spreads or sitting out fix this.

action.plain: Convert any naked-call earnings idea into a debit spread (buy a closer call, sell a further-out call). Or just trade the day after.
action.pro: Robinhood: when you build any earnings options trade, always pick the multi-leg view, not the single-leg, to build a spread.

sell.confidence_reason: Universal rule rather than a single setup; IV crush on naked options post-earnings is mathematically guaranteed regardless of direction, so the confidence is high.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: Top-quality AI semi with earnings 6/03 just outside the CSP expiry, 38.8% annualized on a 6.2% OTM put, and RSI 69.5 strong-not-parabolic: capped by 0.4% from 52w high.
wheel_candidates.pros: Mega-cap AI/networking name with structural HBM and custom-silicon demand. Earnings on 6/03 land just outside the 6/05 CSP expiry, so no binary inside the trade. RSI 69.5 is strong but not parabolic, the 50DMA at 349 sits 17% below spot giving real cushion if assigned, and 38.8% annualized on a 6% OTM put is rich for a name this quality.
wheel_candidates.cons: Spot is 0.4% from the 52-week high so the 'cushion' is theoretical until the chart confirms a higher low. Assignment at 395 is still owning a $40 billion-revenue semi at a premium multiple. AI-spend cycle wobble or a tariff headline could drive a sharp gap-down that blows through the 6% buffer in one day.

action.plain: Sell to open one June 5 $395 put on Broadcom for about $13.44 per share. That collects $1,344 today; if the stock stays above $395 by June 5 you keep all of it. If it drops below $395, you buy 100 shares at an effective cost of $381.56 (strike minus premium received).
action.pro: STO 1 AVGO 2026-06-05 395P @ $13.44 mid (lim near mid, GFD). Collateral $39,500. 38.8% annualized, 6.24% OTM, 32 DTE. Roll the put down-and-out if AVGO closes < $400 with broken structure. If assigned, immediately STO covered call against the position at the next ~30 DTE 5% OTM strike.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: Cloud + AI infra story with no earnings inside the trade window, 46.7% annualized on 5.4% OTM, RSI 60.8 mid-band: capped by recent rerating volatility.
wheel_candidates.pros: Oracle's cloud infrastructure + OpenAI capacity deals are the cleanest enterprise-AI revenue story right now. RSI 60.8 is mid-band so the trade isn't fading a parabola. ER on 6/10 sits five days outside the 6/05 expiry, so no print risk inside the CSP. 46.7% annualized on a 5.4% OTM strike is generous for a name this stable.
wheel_candidates.cons: Spot trades below the 200DMA right now: that's the main wheel-quality concern. The post-rerating chart is still digesting and the 50DMA cluster at $155 is the realistic stop-out level if assigned. Capacity-deal headlines can chop violently in either direction during the 32-day window.

action.plain: Sell to open one June 5 $162.50 put on Oracle for about $6.65 per share, collecting $665. If Oracle stays above $162.50, you keep the cash. If assigned, your effective cost is $155.85: close to where the 50-day average sits.
action.pro: STO 1 ORCL 2026-06-05 162.5P @ $6.65 mid (lim, GFD). Collateral $16,250. 46.7% annualized, 5.43% OTM, 32 DTE. Stop alert at $156 (close put if ORCL breaks 50DMA cluster). If assigned, sell 165 or 170 covered call ~30 DTE.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: 76.5% annualized is exceptional, ER 51d outside the trade, but RSI 78 after a 5x run means the premium is pricing real assignment risk: score reflects that asymmetry.
wheel_candidates.pros: Memory-cycle pure-play with HBM demand inflection driving the recent move. 76.5% annualized is the highest yield among quality names and the next earnings on 6/24 is well outside the 6/05 expiry. The 50DMA at 425 leaves real space below assignment.
wheel_candidates.cons: RSI 78 is firmly parabolic and the chart has 5x'd from $96 over the past year. A normal 15-20% pullback in this kind of run lands you assigned $40-80 in the money, not $32 OTM. The 76.5% annualized premium isn't free money: the chain is pricing exactly this kind of mean-reversion risk. Best taken at half size or skipped if you already hold the stock outright (concentration on top of concentration).

action.plain: Sell to open one June 5 $510 put on Micron for about $34.21 per share, collecting $3,421. If Micron stays above $510 by June 5, you keep it all. Be aware that the strike is far enough below today's price that an assignment means owning Micron near $476, a level that requires the AI memory story to keep working.
action.pro: STO 1 MU 2026-06-05 510P @ $34.21 mid (lim, GFD). Collateral $51,000. 76.5% annualized, 5.94% OTM, 32 DTE. Half-size if MU is already a top portfolio position. Roll down-and-out aggressively if MU closes < $500 with vol expansion. If assigned, write 525 or 540 CC ~30 DTE.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: 60.9% annualized with no earnings ever (it's a commodity ETF) is appealing, but assignment means owning a contango-prone futures product: the carry math is different from a stock wheel.
wheel_candidates.pros: Crude oil ETF: no earnings risk by definition, no headline-driven binary. RSI 60.9 sits in the middle of the band. 60.9% annualized on a 6% OTM strike is rich because oil vol has been bid. The instrument has been ranging $115-$145 for 60 days, so the 134 strike sits near the lower band of established support.
wheel_candidates.cons: USO tracks crude futures, not spot oil. In contango markets the negative roll yield bleeds the ETF over time even when oil is flat: assignment locks in that bleed. A geopolitical resolution (Iran deal, OPEC supply add) or a demand-shock recession headline can drop oil 8%+ in a session, blowing through the 6% buffer. Not a 'name you'd want to own forever' the way the wheel typically assumes.

action.plain: Sell to open one June 5 $134 put on the United States Oil Fund for about $7.15 per share, collecting $715. If oil holds and USO stays above $134, keep the premium. If assigned, you own a crude-tracking ETF at an effective $126.85: be ready to write covered calls aggressively to offset the contango drag.
action.pro: STO 1 USO 2026-06-05 134P @ $7.15 mid (lim, GFD). Collateral $13,400. 60.9% annualized, 6.16% OTM. WTI catalyst calendar: OPEC June meeting falls inside the window: size accordingly. If assigned, write CCs aggressively (weekly 2-3% OTM) to fight contango.

wheel_candidates.confidence_reason: 82% annualized is the fattest yield on the board and ER is 80 days out, but RSI 87.2 means selling vol into a top: score reflects that the premium is fat for a reason.
wheel_candidates.pros: 82% annualized is the richest CSP yield in the universe right now. Earnings 7/29 sits well outside the trade window so no binary inside. The foundry-win narrative has been working: INTC is up substantially on real catalysts, not just multiple expansion. The 200DMA at $26 is laughably below spot.
wheel_candidates.cons: RSI 87.2 is textbook parabolic-extension. The premium is fat because every option seller in the chain is pricing the same mean-reversion risk you should be. A normal cooldown to even RSI 60 would mark INTC ~$80, well below the 94 strike. Selling a CSP at the top of a 4x-in-3-months run is fading the trend, not riding it. The wheel premise is 'I'd happily own this': at $94 INTC means owning a fab-builder mid-thesis with ~25 PE.

action.plain: Selling a put on Intel here means collecting $676 in exchange for the obligation to buy 100 shares at $94 if Intel pulls back through that level by June 5. The premium is large because Intel has run hard and the market is pricing a real chance it cools. If you do this, size small: this is a momentum-fade trade dressed up as a wheel.
action.pro: STO 1 INTC 2026-06-05 94P @ $6.76 mid (lim, GFD). Collateral $9,400. 82% annualized, 5.64% OTM. Treat as a 1/4-size position max. Hard stop on the put if INTC closes < $93 (likely cascade once parabolic break). Better setup: wait for an RSI < 70 reset, then re-evaluate.
